behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the handoff between dependency extraction and the scanner input set. A unit test of only the parser can miss dropped transitive dependencies during module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scanner end-to-end locally on a fixture project (manifest + lockfile) with known direct/transitive deps, intercept outbound query payloads via a local stub API, and assert every expected dependency/version is present in the checked set.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real API contract semantics for version-range matching. A mocked API can falsely pass even if the live service ignores or interprets version constraints differently.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real vulnerability DB endpoint in a controlled test account/dataset, query dependencies where the same package has vulnerable and non-vulnerable version ranges; verify returned CVEs match only manifest-declared versions/ranges.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic ordering logic within one function/object boundary and has no required external interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Feed an unsorted in-memory CVE list with mixed severities and CVSS ties into the sorter and assert exact output order by severity rank then CVSS descending.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failures usually occur across parse-edit-serialize modules (formatting, key order, unrelated field mutation), not just in a single setter function.
behaviors.test_description: Apply the bump operation to real fixture manifests (package.json/requirements variants) and compare before/after snapshots: only target dependency version changes; all other entries/structure remain byte- or semantically-equivalent per format rules.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: This requires real Git hosting API and repository behavior (branch creation, auth, PR creation, diff generation). Mocking can hide real integration failures.
behaviors.test_description: In a real test repository, run scanner auto-fix for one vulnerable dependency and assert: PR exists remotely, title/body metadata is correct, and changed files show exactly one dependency-version edit with no extra changes.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver major-bump detection is deterministic local logic; no I/O or cross-module infrastructure is required to expose the core failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run table-driven tests over semver pairs (including prerelease/build metadata cases) and assert breaking-change flag is true only for major-version increases per policy.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: The failure is at module boundaries: semver decision must gate PR module and propagate status to reporting output. Unit tests on either module alone can miss orchestration bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner workflow locally with a fixture that triggers a major bump, using a local fake PR client and real reporter wiring; assert no PR create call is made and output contains the expected review-required flag/reason.
